Since the only kind of statements allowed in a canonical dataflow region are variable declarations and function calls, the compiler may not be able to correctly handle the region (fft_16_ssr1_i16_c16_t16.cpp:173:9)

Multiple burst reads of variable length and bit width 512 has been inferred on bundle 'gmem1'. These burst requests might be further partitioned into multiple requests during RTL generation, based on max_read_burst_length or max_write_burst_length settings. (fft_16_ssr1_i16_c16_t16.cpp:53:22)

An internal stream 'input_stream' with default size can result in deadlock. Please consider resizing the stream using the directive 'set_directive_stream' or the 'HLS stream' pragma.

Finished Loop, function and other optimizations: CPU user time: 0.08 seconds. CPU system time: 0.04 seconds. Elapsed time: 0.12 seconds; current allocated memory: 269.523 MB.
Process fft<fft_params> is made part of the output synchronization in its dataflow region, because it has an external FIFO output and no PIPO output
Process fft<fft_params> has both a successor and writes an output to its caller (see the GUI dataflow viewer). This may lead to lower throughput. Consider copying this output via a successor process.

Cosimulation may deadlock if process fft<fft_params> has a streamed top-level array input and has predecessor processes. If a deadlock occurs, please consider converting the streamed array into an hls::stream

Cosimulation may deadlock if process fft_serial_kernel has a streamed top-level array input and has predecessor processes. If a deadlock occurs, please consider converting the streamed array into an hls::stream
